_doc: Minimal smoke-test config designed to exercise the intra-tick chunk-split path (cache v8). spawnBurst with 200_000 entities in a single tick produces ~200K EcsSpawn events + transaction/page-cache chains within that one tick — well past the IntraTickEventCap threshold of 100_000, forcing the builder's mid-tick flush logic to fire at least once (probably 2-3 times). The resulting cache sidecar will contain multiple chunks sharing the same [fromTick, toTick) range, with FlagIsContinuation set on all but the first. Loading this trace in the viewer exercises the client-side merge (mergeTickData + assembleTickViewAndNumbers' merged-tick detection loop) on a real workload — if any of that machinery is broken, it shows up here first. Use: dotnet run -c Release -- ioprofile-split-test.json.

database._doc: 128 MB cache — same as the 2k variant. 200K Versioned entities at ~112 B each = ~22 MB of live component data, plus revision chains + index pages; comfortably under 128 MB so no backpressure stalls fire.

workload._doc: Three stages: 5-tick ramp-up via spawnCheckpointLoop (gets a few clean ticks on the wire for comparison), one giant tick via spawnBurst (the intra-tick-split trigger), a terminal checkpoint. Total ~8 ticks — tiny trace file, fast to generate and load.

spawnCheckpointLoop._comment: 5 tiny normal ticks before the big one. Lets the viewer see 'normal' tick density adjacent to the split tick, for visual side-by-side comparison.

spawnBurst._comment: 200K entities in ONE tick via 400 batched commits. Expected event output in this tick: ~200K EcsSpawn + ~400 TransactionCommit + ~400 TransactionPersist + BTreeInsert × (200K entities × 1-2 indexes) + PageCache events as the versioned storage allocates pages. Easily > 100_000 events — at least 2 intra-tick splits expected. This IS the whole point of the config.

profile._doc: Same opt-in set as the 2k variant so record shapes + fold behaviour match.
